name: Hanzala ibn As'ad
title: The Brave Preacher
shortDescription: A sincere Shia and Quran reciter from Kufa who preached to the enemies with Quranic verses on Ashura and shielded the Imam.
fullStory:
Hanzala ibn As'ad al-Shibami was a well-known figure, orator, Quran reciter, and sincere Shia in Kufa. He belonged to the Shibam clan of the Hamdan tribe and held a special position among his people. When Imam Hussain (AS) reached Karbala, Hanzala joined the Imam's caravan and became one of his close companions and preachers of his army.

On the day of Ashura, before the hand-to-hand combat escalated, Hanzala asked Imam Hussain (AS) for permission to go to the battlefield and speak to the Kufan army. He stood before Umar Sa'd's army and recited verses from Surah Ghafir with a loud voice: 'O my people! Indeed I fear for you the like of the day of the companies... O my people! Indeed I fear for you the Day of Calling'.

After reciting the verses, Hanzala shouted: 'O people! Do not kill Hussain, lest God destroy you with a severe punishment'. His logical and Quranic words were so impactful that Imam Hussain (AS) praised him and said: 'O Ibn As'ad, may God have mercy on you. They deserved punishment the moment they rejected your call and shed the blood of your companions'.

After this epic sermon and completing the argument with the enemies, Hanzala turned to the Imam and said: 'Is it not time to go to our Lord?' The Imam said: 'Go towards what is better than the world and whatever is in it'. Hanzala bravely charged into the heart of the enemy army.

He fought with great skill and, after killing a group of enemies, was targeted by arrows and spears and was martyred. His name is also mentioned with respect in the Ziyarat of Nahiya al-Muqaddasa.
quoteArabic: يا قَوْمِ، لا تَقْتُلُوا الحُسَيْنَ فَيُسْحِتَكُمُ اللَّهُ بِعَذابٍ
quote: O people! Do not kill Hussain, lest God destroy you with a severe punishment.
